title: Bradley Kim becomes first openly gay US military-academy football player
description: Air Force Academy player Bradley Kim came out as gay, making him the first openly gay football player at any United States military academy.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mike Jacobs is first sitting US judge to come out as bisexual
description: Judge Mike Jacobs came out as bisexual, the first sitting judge in the United States known to have done so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: California designates official LGBTQ veterans memorial
description: A California law named the LGBTQ Veterans Memorial at Desert Memorial Park the state's official LGBTQ veterans memorial, making California the first US state to formally recognize LGBTQ military veterans.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tadd Fujikawa becomes first openly gay male professional golfer
description: Professional golfer Tadd Fujikawa came out as gay in an Instagram post, the first male pro golfer to do so publicly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Yance Ford is first openly transgender man to win an Emmy
description: Yance Ford and Joslyn Barnes received an Emmy for Exceptional Merit in Documentary Filmmaking for Strong Island. Ford thereby became the first openly transgender man, and the first Black openly transgender person, to win an Emmy.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lord Ivar Mountbatten has first same-sex wedding in British royal family circle
description: Lord Ivar Mountbatten married James Coyle on 22 September 2018, the first same-sex wedding within the extended family of the British monarch.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: West Hollywood holds first citywide Bi Pride in the United States
description: West Hollywood hosted the first citywide Bi Pride event in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Patricio Manuel is first openly transgender man to box professionally in the US
description: Patricio Manuel became the first openly transgender man to compete in a professional boxing match in the United States, and by winning the bout also the first to win one.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First same-sex kiss at the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade
description: Cast members of the musical The Prom closed their Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade performance with the parade's first same-sex kiss.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ariel Nicholson is first openly transgender model in a Calvin Klein show
description: Model Ariel Nicholson walked in a Calvin Klein fashion show, the first openly transgender model to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jacinda Ardern is first New Zealand prime minister to march in a pride parade
description: Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern marched in a gay pride parade, the first sitting head of government of New Zealand to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Toni Atkins is first openly LGBT leader of the California State Senate
description: Toni Atkins took over from Kevin de León as Senate President Pro Tempore, becoming both the first woman and the first openly LGBT person to lead the California State Senate.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Christine Hallquist is first openly transgender major-party nominee for governor
description: Democrats in Vermont nominated Christine Hallquist for governor, making her the first openly transgender gubernatorial candidate of a major party in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kyrsten Sinema wins first major-party US Senate nomination as an openly bisexual person
description: Kyrsten Sinema secured a major-party nomination for a US Senate seat, the first openly bisexual person to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kyrsten Sinema is first openly bisexual person elected to the US Senate
description: Kyrsten Sinema of Arizona won her race for the US Senate, becoming the first openly bisexual person elected to that chamber.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Graça Fonseca is Portugal's first openly gay government minister
description: Graça Fonseca joined the Portuguese government as its first openly gay minister.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Xavier Bettel is first openly gay prime minister re-elected to a second term
description: Luxembourg's Xavier Bettel won re-election, making him the first openly gay prime minister anywhere to secure a second term.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Etienne Schneider re-elected as openly gay deputy prime minister
description: Etienne Schneider of Luxembourg became the first openly gay deputy prime minister in the world to win re-election for a second term.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jared Polis is first openly gay person elected governor of a US state
description: Jared Polis won the governorship of Colorado, the first openly gay person elected governor of any US state. Jim McGreevey had come out while in office in 2004, and Kate Brown, who is bisexual, was elected in Oregon in 2016 as the first openly LGBT governor.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sharice Davids is first openly gay Native American elected to US Congress
description: Sharice Davids won election to the US Congress as the first openly gay Native American member, and the first openly LGBTQ person in the Kansas congressional delegation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Angie Craig is first openly lesbian mother elected to US Congress
description: Angie Craig of Minnesota won her congressional race, becoming the first openly lesbian mother in the US Congress and the first openly gay member elected from Minnesota.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ahmad Zahra is first openly gay Muslim elected to US public office
description: Ahmad Zahra won a seat on the Fullerton city council in California, the first openly gay Muslim elected to public office in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chris Pappas is first openly gay member of Congress from New Hampshire
description: Chris Pappas won election to the US House, the first openly gay person to represent New Hampshire in Congress.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Katie Hill is California's first openly bisexual member of Congress
description: Katie Hill won her House race, becoming the first openly bisexual person and first openly queer woman elected to Congress from California.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ricardo Lara is first openly gay person elected statewide in California
description: Ricardo Lara won election as California's insurance commissioner, the first openly gay person elected to statewide office in the state's history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Megan Hunt is first openly LGBTQ person elected to Nebraska's legislature
description: Megan Hunt, who is openly bisexual, won election to the Nebraska state legislature as its first openly LGBTQ member.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Angela Ponce is first openly transgender woman crowned Miss Spain
description: Angela Ponce won the Miss Spain title on 29 June 2018 as the first openly transgender titleholder, and went on to become the first openly transgender contestant at Miss Universe.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Laverne Cox is first openly transgender person on a Cosmopolitan cover
description: Laverne Cox appeared on the cover of Cosmopolitan South Africa's February 2018 issue, the first openly transgender person on the cover of any Cosmopolitan edition.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Paris Lees is first openly transgender woman featured in British Vogue
description: British Vogue featured Paris Lees, the first openly transgender woman to appear in the magazine.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First documented case of a transgender woman breastfeeding
description: The journal Transgender Health reported that a transgender woman in the United States breastfed her adopted baby, the first known documented case of its kind.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jessica Platt is first out transgender woman in North American pro hockey
description: Canadian Women's Hockey League player Jessica Platt came out as a transgender woman, the first player in North American professional hockey to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Yance Ford is first openly transgender man nominated for an Academy Award
description: Yance Ford and Joslyn Barnes were nominated for the Best Documentary Feature Oscar for Strong Island, which Ford also directed. Ford was the first openly transgender man, and the first openly transgender director, nominated for any Academy Award.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Daniela Vega is first openly transgender presenter at the Academy Awards
description: Chilean actress Daniela Vega presented at the Academy Awards ceremony, the first openly transgender person to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Marvia Malik is first openly transgender newsreader on Pakistani television
description: Marvia Malik went on air as a newsreader, the first openly transgender person to present the news on Pakistani television.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Peppermint is first trans woman to originate a principal Broadway role
description: Peppermint made her Broadway debut as Pythio in the musical Head Over Heels, which opened on 26 July 2018 after previews from 23 June. She became the first trans woman to originate a principal role on Broadway.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Colombia prosecutes a transgender woman's murder as femicide for the first time
description: A Colombian court treated the killing of Anyela Ramos Claros, a transgender woman, as aggravated femicide for the first time, sentencing Davinson Stiven Erazo Sánchez to twenty years in a psychiatric facility.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jesse James Keitel plays TV Land's first non-binary character
description: Jesse James Keitel appeared on the series Younger as the first non-binary character shown on TV Land.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lucy Clark becomes first openly transgender football referee
description: British referee Lucy Clark came out as a transgender woman, making her the first openly transgender referee in the sport's history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Austria
description: Austria's marriage equality took effect, following the 2017 Constitutional Court ruling that ordered access to civil marriage for same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Taiwan legalizes same-sex marriage, the first in Asia
description: Taiwan passed same-sex marriage legislation that also took effect the same year, making it the first jurisdiction in Asia with marriage equality.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Three Mexican states enact same-sex marriage
description: The Mexican states of San Luis Potosí, Hidalgo and Baja California Sur passed same-sex marriage laws that also came into force in 2019.
region: San Luis Potosí, Hidalgo, Baja California Sur

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ecuador's highest court rules for same-sex marriage
description: Ecuador's Constitutional Court ruled in favor of same-sex marriage, opening civil marriage to same-sex couples nationwide.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Court rulings bring same-sex marriage to Nuevo León and Aguascalientes
description: Supreme court rulings extended same-sex marriage to the Mexican states of Nuevo León and Aguascalientes.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Civil unions take effect in San Marino
description: San Marino's civil union law came into force, giving same-sex couples legal recognition of their partnerships.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Cayman Islands court order on same-sex unions stayed pending appeal
description: A court in the Cayman Islands, a British overseas territory, ordered legal recognition for same-sex couples, but the ruling remained unimplemented while under appeal.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Angola decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: Angola adopted a new penal code that removed the colonial-era provisions used against same-sex conduct, decriminalizing homosexuality.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Botswana decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: Botswana's High Court struck down the laws criminalizing consensual same-sex relations, decriminalizing homosexuality in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Brunei brings harsh new penalties for same-sex acts into force
description: Brunei put new penal-code provisions into effect that impose severe punishments, including death by stoning, for same-sex acts.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gabon criminalizes same-sex relations
description: Gabon adopted legislation criminalizing consensual same-sex relations for the first time.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Canada equalizes the age of consent
description: Canada repealed the separate, higher age of consent that had applied to anal intercourse, equalizing the age of consent for all sexual activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bermuda equalizes the age of consent
description: Bermuda, a British overseas territory, brought its age of consent for same-sex activity into line with that for different-sex activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
